reviews.review_msg: Very disappointing course. I get the impression that Irene just doesn't have a very firm grasp on the material and it bleeds through to the lectures where often she goes around in circles or on tangents about unrelated material, or her personal life. Maybe she's better in other courses but she clearly was out of her depth here.

I can tell she cares about students, but the disorganization of this class makes it almost impossible to follow.Her lectures often left me more confused than before. The midterm was excessively confusing! It took SO LONG to get them back.She gets easily sidetracked and for about 30 mins of class all we would do is listen to her ramble. Never again.

I personally found the grading quite strict and expectations sometimes unclear. The midterm format was a bit confusing and felt rushed for the time given. Assignment instructions could be more detailed. Lectures weren't always very engaging for me.

the only reason is300 is required is because no one would ever take a class with her unless required to. Never got any grades back, lectures barely covered course material, and she has poor time management.

She's a nice prof, but half the time her explanations don't make sense. She gets sidetracked often and doesn't clarify concepts well compared to how she phrases them on exams. The midterm MC was more confusing than necessary.

Slides don't match the reading guides and it is impossible to get a solid grasp on the concepts. When explaining some of the concepts, she confuses herself and attempts to backtrack. Typically just leaves you more confused.

Prof Pang is a brilliant (and best) teacher who is aware of the best pedagogical approaches and cares for her students. There is a relevance of what we learn to our lives and practices, she has taught us how to be a better consumer of knowledge and a better producer of knowledge. She is a tough grader but gives clear feedback and you learn a lot.

Professor Pang is educated and very well-read. Her lectures were highly enjoyable and informative and really shapes how you view capitalism and anti-capitalism. Reading responses are opportunities for easy marks and all major essays are scaled. Doesn't mark on the international studies' program's 5% scale is a plus! Overall loved the course!
If you want a course in order to understand this life and how the world works politically and economically, I recommend it. Deep readings and educated professor

She is a tough marker but she scales the grades so even if you don't do great on a paper it won't be reflected in your overall mark. There are weekly reading guide assignments that help with tutorials and are an easy grade booster. If you want an easy A this isn't the class for you, but if you want actually learn something it's for you.

She is extremely opinionated so the class does not feel like an independent place to learn but rather a place to learn her opinions. The workload is too much with two deadlines a week and four papers just for one class.

Not great at teaching, but she's open to suggestions & will change something if the majority of the class is in agreement. This course is dry, v heavy & she lectures for the entire four hours online. Offers bonus points, but is a tough grader and her marking can be unclear. If you're not keen on talking in class she won't force you.

I think I learned a lot in this class. Lots of readings required, but interesting and worthwhile. Grading was curved which was nice since she's a tough marker. I don't feel she's the most effective professor and sometimes made the concepts confusing to understand. She's also quite the stickler for keeping cameras on.

Professor Pang is an amazing prof who cares about her students and is quite accessible for support outside class as well. I enjoyed her teaching style as she delivered great lectures that were engaging. Shes a tough marker so be aware of that, however, be on top of the readings and assignments and you should be fine.

One of the worst courses I've taken at SFU. Clearly has no idea how to teach, asks for feedback and then is rude/refuses to implement, can't keep up with the workload she assigns, gives no criteria for assignments, has no rubric and relies on a check mark system she refused to explain. Most unclear syllabus I've seen in 5 years, wish I could give 0

While she delivers decent lectures, the biggest problem with this professor is her poor attitude that makes it difficult you to approach her for help. Never experienced this with other IS professors. Combined with her tough grading, you'll be in for a depressing ride.

I cannot express the trauma I sustained from this class. We spent all semester working on a group research proposal except the group was the whole class. Yes there were 34 of us contributing to a single document. Almost an hour of every lecture was spent asking her what was going on. I still dont know how were getting graded. Im so confused.

Im writing this to warn people choosing classes soon. This professor isnt tough because of difficult lecture and course content, but cuz her instructing skills are so poor. Doesnt know how to create a proper rubric or give proper feedback. Everything is unclear and confusing + still marked very hard  impossible to do well. Being as fair as I can.
